name: Get Pending GTT Order
summary: Retrieves the list of pending GTT (Good Till Triggered) alert orders for the logged-in user. POST call with jData (JSON object) and jKey (login session key) as request parameters; returns an array of pending GTT order objects.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid*
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: Any message Entered during order entry.

response_fields.name: d
response_fields.description: Data to be compared with LTP

response_fields.name: trantype*
response_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

response_fields.name: ret*
response_fields.description: Retention type [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject]

response_fields.name: qty*
response_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

response_fields.name: prc*
response_fields.description: Order Price [If prc is junk value other than numbers] "Order price cannot be zero".

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired

notes: Request parameters jData and jKey are sent as URL form-encoded data (jData=...&jKey=...), where jData is the JSON object. The production base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/ (the curl sample uses a 'BaseURL' placeholder). The only request json_field is uid (mandatory). Field-to-possible-value bindings verified against the page-34 image: validity=DAY or GTT; trantype*=B/S; prctyp=LMT / SL-LMT / DS / 2L / 3L (the possible-value cell wraps across two text lines but is one cell); prd=C/M/H; ret*=DAY/EOS/IOC. The response-fields table spans pages 34-35 (qty*, prc*, emsg continue on page 35). The prctyp response row has a possible-value cell but no description text in the table. The doc lists ret in the response-fields table but it does not appear in the sample success response. The sample success JSON uses mixed-case keys (Al_id, Token, Remarks, Qty, Prc) that differ from the lowercase table field names (al_id, token, remarks, qty, prc), and includes extra keys not in the field table (Qty, Prc, C, ordersource, oivariable) plus a nested oivariable array of {var_name, d}. The mandatory markers on trantype*, ret*, qty*, prc* appear carried over from the order-entry/GTT-placement field definitions even though this is a read operation.
issues: Mixed-case key drift between the field table and the sample JSON: al_id->Al_id, token->Token, remarks->Remarks, qty->Qty, prc->Prc. The prctyp possible-value cell wraps across two text lines ("LMT / SL-LMT / DS" then "/ 2L / 3L") but the image confirms it is one cell reading "LMT / SL-LMT / DS / 2L / 3L"; prctyp has no description in the table. Several mandatory-marked response fields (trantype*, ret*, qty*, prc*) are inconsistent with a read operation but are transcribed verbatim from the doc. ret appears in the response table but not in the sample success response.
